name: Fish Soup (Kakavia)
recipeInstructions:
1. Obtain fish trimmings such as heads and backbones from the fishmonger.
2. Rinse well and place in a large pot.
3. Add water, salt, and 1/2 teaspoon peppercorns.
4. Simmer for 2 hours.
5. Strain the stock and reserve.
6. Gently fry onion, garlic, chopped leeks, carrots, and celery in olive oil for 10 minutes.
7. Add chopped tomatoes, herbs tied into a bunch, and reserved fish stock.
8. Season with salt and freshly ground pepper.
9. Cover and simmer for 30 minutes.
10. Add potatoes and boil for 5 minutes.
11. Reduce heat, add fish pieces, and simmer for 3 minutes.
12. Add lobster pieces and prawns; simmer for 7-10 minutes until prawns turn pink and lobster meat is cooked.
13. Do not boil when shellfish are added, as they will toughen.
14. Pile fish, shellfish, and potatoes in a serving dish.
15. Sprinkle with chopped parsley and keep hot.
16. Serve soup in a tureen as a first course with crusty bread.
17. Follow with seafoods and potatoes served with lemon wedges and a cruet of olive oil.
18. Alternatively, serve soup, potatoes, and seafoods together in deep plates.
NOTE: Suitable fish include snapper, mullet, eel, whiting, john dory, and bream. Cut into 5 cm (2 inch) slices and sprinkle with lemon juice. Add heads and trimmings to the fish stock. Cut rinsed lobster tails into 5 cm (2 inch) pieces with shell on. Shell and de-vein prawns.
